page.primary_keyword: marbella yacht charter age policy kids
page.title: Marbella Yacht Charter Age Policy for Kids: Minimum Ages & Safety Rules

data.meta_description: Learn the minimum age policy for kids on a Marbella yacht charter. Safety rules, life jackets & requirements for children on boats in Puerto Banús.
data.summary: This page explains the age policy for children on a Marbella yacht charter, covering minimum ages, safety rules, and life jacket requirements for families renting boats in Puerto Banús.
key_facts: No minimum age for infants, but life jackets must be worn at all times on deck.
Children under 12 must wear a life jacket when outside the cabin.
Our fleet carries child-sized life jackets (up to 50 kg) on request.
Skipper has final authority on safety; rough weather may restrict children.
All charters include licensed skipper, insurance, and safety briefing.
Private charters allow families full control over itinerary and pace.
Book via WhatsApp to confirm age requirements and jacket sizes.

faq.q: What is the minimum age for children on a Marbella yacht charter?
faq.a: There is no official minimum age for infants on our charters. However, children under 12 must wear a life jacket when on deck. The skipper may advise against infants in rough sea conditions for safety.

faq.q: Do you provide life jackets for kids?
faq.a: Yes, we stock child-sized life jackets (suitable up to 50 kg) on all boats. Request sizes when booking via WhatsApp so we can prepare the right fit. Adults have life jackets onboard too.

faq.q: Can children go on jet skis or water toys?
faq.a: Jet skis are included free on Mangusta 80 charters (minimum 4 hours), but riders must be at least 16 years old with a valid licence if driving. Children can ride as passengers with a licensed adult. Towed toys like inflatables are available on request.

faq.q: What safety rules apply to kids during the charter?
faq.a: Children must wear life jackets on deck at all times. The skipper gives a safety briefing at departure. Kids should stay seated while underway, and no running or jumping from the boat without skipper approval. Parents remain responsible for supervision.

faq.q: Is the yacht charter private? Can we bring our own nanny?
faq.a: Yes, all our charters are private — just your group plus the skipper. You may bring a nanny or extra adult; the boat's maximum passenger limit (e.g., 12 pax on Mangusta 80) must not be exceeded. Check capacity on the boat page.

faq.q: What if the weather is bad – can we still bring kids?
faq.a: The skipper assesses conditions on the day. In rough seas or high wind, the skipper may recommend rescheduling or limiting children's time on deck. Safety is priority; we can usually reschedule without penalty.

faq.q: Are there any age restrictions for swimming stops?
faq.a: No age restrictions for swimming, but children must wear life jackets in the water unless the skipper deems it safe otherwise. We anchor in calm coves like Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés, ideal for families. Parents must supervise at all times.

faq.q: How do I book a family-friendly yacht charter in Marbella?
faq.a: Contact us via WhatsApp with your group size, children's ages, and preferred date. We'll recommend a boat from our fleet (e.g., Azimut 58 Flybridge or Mangusta 80) and confirm life jacket sizes. Pricing starts from €749/2h for boats like Astondoa 40.

data.body_html:
Planning a family day on the water in Marbella? Knowing the Marbella yacht charter age policy for kids is essential before you book. Whether you're cruising from Puerto Banús with toddlers or teenagers, we set clear safety rules and provide the right equipment so everyone enjoys the Mediterranean safely. Here's what you need to know about minimum ages, life jackets, and skipper guidance on our fleet.

Minimum Age for Kids on a Marbella Yacht Charter

There is no official minimum age for infants on our charters. Babies and toddlers are welcome aboard, provided they wear a life jacket when on deck. For safety, the skipper may advise against very young children in rough sea conditions (winds above 15 knots or waves over 1.5 m). All our boats depart from Puerto Banús, where the marina is sheltered and calm for boarding.

Life Jacket Requirements for Children

Children under 12 must wear a life jacket at all times when outside the cabin. We stock child-sized life jackets (suitable up to 50 kg) on every boat — simply request sizes when booking via WhatsApp. Adults have life jackets onboard too, but they are not mandatory unless conditions require. The skipper will demonstrate proper fitting during the safety briefing.

Safety Briefing and Skipper Authority

Every charter includes a licensed skipper who gives a full safety briefing before departure. The briefing covers life jacket locations, emergency procedures, and rules for kids (no running, stay seated while underway). The skipper has final authority on safety decisions — including limiting children's deck access in rough weather. Our skippers are experienced with families and will adjust the itinerary to suit young guests.

Jet Skis, Water Toys, and Age Limits

On our Mangusta 80 charters (minimum 4 hours), a jet ski is included free of charge. Riders must be at least 16 years old and hold a valid licence to drive. Children can ride as passengers with a licensed adult. Towed inflatables (e.g., doughnuts) are available on request — kids must wear life jackets and be supervised by an adult. Other boats like the Azimut 58 Flybridge can accommodate water toys upon prior arrangement.

Swimming Stops and Supervision

We anchor in family-friendly coves such as Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés, where the water is calm and shallow. Children can swim with life jackets on — the skipper will advise if it's safe to remove them. Parents remain responsible for supervision at all times. The boat's swim platform makes getting in and out easy for little ones.

Private Charters: Full Control Over Your Day

All our charters are private — just your group plus the skipper. This means you set the pace, choose the itinerary, and decide when to stop for lunch or swim. No strangers onboard. Maximum passenger limits apply (e.g., 12 pax on Ferretti 94 or Mangusta 80), so check the boat page for capacity. You can bring a nanny or extra adult as long as the limit isn't exceeded.

Booking for Families: What to Tell Us

When you book a Marbella yacht charter via WhatsApp, please provide the ages of all children and any specific requirements (e.g., life jacket sizes, dietary needs for snacks). We'll recommend the best boat from our fleet — for example, the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6 m, 12 pax) offers plenty of shade and a spacious saloon for toddlers. Pricing starts from €749/2h for boats like the Astondoa 40 (12.5 m, 9 pax).

Weather and Rescheduling for Kids

If the forecast shows rough seas or strong wind, the skipper may recommend rescheduling — especially with young children. We can usually move your booking to another day without penalty. Safety is our priority, and we'd rather you have a calm, enjoyable trip than a bumpy one. Check the age policy page for updates.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the minimum age for children on a Marbella yacht charter?

There is no official minimum age for infants on our charters. However, children under 12 must wear a life jacket when on deck. The skipper may advise against infants in rough sea conditions for safety.

Do you provide life jackets for kids?

Yes, we stock child-sized life jackets (suitable up to 50 kg) on all boats. Request sizes when booking via WhatsApp so we can prepare the right fit. Adults have life jackets onboard too.

Can children go on jet skis or water toys?

Jet skis are included free on Mangusta 80 charters (minimum 4 hours), but riders must be at least 16 years old with a valid licence if driving. Children can ride as passengers with a licensed adult. Towed toys like inflatables are available on request.

What safety rules apply to kids during the charter?

Children must wear life jackets on deck at all times. The skipper gives a safety briefing at departure. Kids should stay seated while underway, and no running or jumping from the boat without skipper approval. Parents remain responsible for supervision.

Is the yacht charter private? Can we bring our own nanny?

Yes, all our charters are private — just your group plus the skipper. You may bring a nanny or extra adult; the boat's maximum passenger limit (e.g., 12 pax on Mangusta 80) must not be exceeded. Check capacity on the boat page.

What if the weather is bad – can we still bring kids?

The skipper assesses conditions on the day. In rough seas or high wind, the skipper may recommend rescheduling or limiting children's time on deck. Safety is priority; we can usually reschedule without penalty.

Are there any age restrictions for swimming stops?

No age restrictions for swimming, but children must wear life jackets in the water unless the skipper deems it safe otherwise. We anchor in calm coves like Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés, ideal for families. Parents must supervise at all times.

How do I book a family-friendly yacht charter in Marbella?

Contact us via WhatsApp with your group size, children's ages, and preferred date. We'll recommend a boat from our fleet (e.g., Azimut 58 Flybridge or Mangusta 80) and confirm life jacket sizes. Pricing starts from €749/2h for boats like Astondoa 40.
